name: mitre-itk
description: 27 MITRE Innovation Toolkit skills adapted for product practitioners — systems thinking, HCI-rooted design, and stakeholder work across 5 innovation phases.

plugins.name: itk-community-map
plugins.description: A fast way to capture and prioritize stakeholders. Best for workshop openers and rapid stakeholder discovery.

plugins.name: itk-culture-building-canvas
plugins.description: A framework based on influence channels that shape and define an organization's culture. Best for team formation, org design, and culture change initiatives.

plugins.name: itk-stakeholder-quickstart
plugins.description: A quick way to begin developing a plan for effectively engaging a stakeholder. Best for individual stakeholder prep before key meetings.

plugins.name: itk-stakeholder-identify
plugins.description: Ideate a more comprehensive and representative set of relevant stakeholders to your project. Best for discovery kickoffs and initiative scoping.

plugins.name: itk-stakeholder-map
plugins.description: Categorize stakeholders by key variables (interest, influence, impact) and build an actionable engagement plan. Best for stakeholder prioritization and migration planning.

plugins.name: itk-stakeholder-power
plugins.description: Quickly categorize and prioritize which stakeholders to engage based on power and impact. Best for navigating org politics and elevating underrepresented voices.

plugins.name: itk-system-map
plugins.description: Captures the key roles, relationships, and dynamics of a system to enhance understanding of the big picture. Best for complex multi-stakeholder products and systems thinking.

plugins.name: itk-mission-vision
plugins.description: Develop clear statements about your organization's future aspirations and present activities. Best for team alignment at the start of a quarter or initiative.

plugins.name: itk-premortem
plugins.description: Imagine a future failure to uncover risks and strengthen plans before committing. Best for stress-testing OKRs, roadmaps, and launch plans.

plugins.name: itk-problem-framing
plugins.description: Explore a problem space and formulate a robust problem statement to ensure you're solving the right problem. Best for discovery kickoffs and PRD problem sections.

plugins.name: itk-card-sorting
plugins.description: Participants communicate their mental model of how they think about a set of information, creating logical structure. Best for IA work, feature naming, and navigation design.

plugins.name: itk-journey-mapping
plugins.description: Visually synthesize a user's end-to-end experience through actions, pain points, wins, and opportunities. Best for discovery synthesis and roadmap prioritization.

plugins.name: itk-painstorming
plugins.description: Structured method for gathering user insights using Persona, Activities, Insights, and Needs (PAIN). Best for early-stage discovery and user story development.

plugins.name: itk-personas
plugins.description: A descriptive model of a person used to help a team define and understand the needs of its customer. Best for aligning cross-functional teams on who the product actually serves.

plugins.name: itk-service-blueprint
plugins.description: Visualize a service by connecting the user experience frontstage to what happens backstage. Best for identifying handoff failures and cross-team dependencies.

plugins.name: itk-storyboarding
plugins.description: Add a human element to design and data analysis activities through narrative and visual storytelling. Best for communicating user context to stakeholders and engineering.

plugins.name: itk-value-prop
plugins.description: Map user profiles (jobs, pains, gains) to product values (gain creators, pain relievers) to ensure product-market fit. Best for PRD value sections and positioning work.

plugins.name: itk-bodystorming
plugins.description: Physical brainstorming where participants role-play and mimic interaction with a product or experience. Best for interaction design and physical/spatial product work.

plugins.name: itk-lotus-blossom
plugins.description: Focus brainstorming using a structured, visual grid that pushes teams to generate ideas across every cell. Best for deep ideation on a focused problem.

plugins.name: itk-mindmapping
plugins.description: Generate insights into relationships between concepts in a flexible, visual way. Best for early exploration and surfacing unexpected connections.

plugins.name: itk-triz-prism
plugins.description: Use known solutions from other domains to find new solutions to difficult problems through analogical reasoning. Best for stuck teams and novel constraint problems.

plugins.name: itk-prototyping
plugins.description: Develop an early version of a product to convey look and feel; built quickly to test hypotheses before committing. Best for de-risking features before sprint commitment.

plugins.name: itk-retro-rundown
plugins.description: Structured, facilitated team reflection comparing expected vs. actual outcomes and identifying concrete improvements. Best for sprint retrospectives and post-launch reviews.

plugins.name: itk-rose-bud-thorn
plugins.description: Visually categorize positive (rose), potential (bud), and negative (thorn) aspects of a topic. Best for lightweight retrospectives and mid-sprint check-ins.

plugins.name: itk-simplicity-cycle
plugins.description: Visual vocabulary for discussing complexity vs. value in a design — helps teams assess whether adding or removing elements improves the product. Best for scope management and design reviews.

plugins.name: itk-stormdraining
plugins.description: Reduce a large collection of ideas to the most valuable or promising. The inverse of brainstorming. Best for converging after ideation sessions.

plugins.name: itk-trimming
plugins.description: Iteratively remove unnecessary elements from a design using a structured approach to reducing complexity. Best for scope cuts, feature pruning, and simplification sprints.
